title: ophioglossum vulgatum subsp. africanum
intro: Ophioglossum vulgatum subsp. africanum is a subspecies of the fern plant, also known as the adder's tongue, that is native to tropical Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Ophioglossum vulgatum subsp. africanum include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. In herbal preparations, Ophioglossum vulgatum subsp. africanum is often used as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies such as poultices and ointments.
